news.news: The European Commission has approved Amgen's IMDYLLTRA (tarlatamab), a T-cell engager bispecific antibody, for the treatment of extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC). The approval is based on the Phase 3 DeLLphi-304 trial, which demonstrated a 40% reduction in the risk of death compared to chemotherapy.

AstraZeneca presented updated results from the Phase 3 SERENA-6 trial at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting, showing that the camizestrant combination significantly delayed time to first and second progression in patients with advanced HR-positive breast cancer with an ESR1 mutation. The study also demonstrated profound reduction in circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA).

UCB presented new long-term data at EULAR 2026 for BIMZELX (bimekizumab), a dual IL-17A and IL-17F inhibitor monoclonal antibody. The data showed that early inflammation control with bimekizumab in psoriatic arthritis and axial spondyloarthritis led to sustained improvements in patient-reported outcomes and reduced radiographic progression over three years.

Junshi Biosciences announced that the primary endpoints of event-free survival and major pathological response were met in the final analysis of the Phase 3 NEOTORCH study for toripalimab, a PD-1 monoclonal antibody, combined with chemotherapy for resectable stage II-III non-small cell lung cancer. The company plans to submit a supplemental new drug application to expand the indication.

The National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) in China approved toripalimab in combination with disitamab vedotin for the first-line treatment of HER2-expressing locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma. The approval is based on the Phase 3 RC48-C016 trial, which showed significant improvements in progression-free and overall survival compared to chemotherapy.
